explanations.17: This pulls the Composer tool from a specific version image and labels it 'composer_base' so we can copy files from it later.
explanations.27: The 'set -e -x' makes the script exit on any error and print each command as it runs, which helps catch problems early.
explanations.29: The '\e[0;105m' and '\e[0;0m' are ANSI color codes that make the output text appear in bright magenta for visibility during the build.
explanations.54: This copies the Composer executable from the earlier 'composer_base' image into the current image—we only need the final tool, not the whole image.
explanations.60: The '--password ''' creates a user with no password set, and '-u ${OS_USERID}' lets you specify the exact numeric ID instead of letting Docker assign one.
explanations.69: After installing PHP extensions, 'docker-php-source delete' cleans up temporary build files to keep the image smaller.
explanations.71: The '/var/list/apt/*' path looks suspicious—it should probably be '/var/lib/apt/*' to properly clean the package manager cache.
